citations.narrative:
An acquisition record for a crested pigeon is incomplete. One adult crested pigeon was obtained on 03 February 2026
from a non-USDA licensed individual and the record did not have the vehicle license number and State, and the driver's
license number and State of the person. Records containing the required information for animals disposed of, or acquired
for, use in regulated activities (including those offspring born on premises) are necessary to accurately track animals to
ensure their humane care and use. The licensee must make and maintain records of the inventory, acquisition and
disposition of all animals which fully and correctly disclose the required information in this subpart 2.75(b)(1).
Correct from this date forward of 10 March 2026.

Multiple slices of white bread with blue-green discoloration suggesting mold activity are on the ground in an enclosure for
4 domestic chickens. The bread was provided earlier in the day and intended for food. Feeding food with evidence of
contamination such as mold to birds may cause adverse health effects. The diet for birds must be wholesome, palatable,
and free of contamination.
Corrected at time of inspection. Correct from this date forward of 10 March 2026.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
There are 21 breeding pigeons in an enclosure that has multiple cardboard nests encrusted in droppings. The licensee
states the cardboard nests are removed when the parents are no longer actively using them and there is no seasonality to
timing of breeding in this flock. There is no documentation of a modified cleaning schedule or a date when the primary
enclosure was last cleaned. There is also no documentation of when regular cleaning will resume.
Without a written plan to deviate from normal cleaning, inspectors do not know when the last thorough cleaning was
performed, what the deviation in cleaning is during breeding, egg-sitting or feeding of chicks, and when normal cleaning
will resume when it no longer may disrupt breeding, egg-sitting, and/or feeding of chicks. Inadequate cleaning can result
in disease or respiratory irritation in the birds.
Scheduled cleaning may be modified or delayed during breeding, egg-sitting, or feeding of chicks for birds that are easily
disrupted during such behaviors. Scheduled cleaning must resume when such cleaning no longer disrupts breeding, egg-
sitting, or feeding of chicks. A schedule of cleaning must be documented and must include when breeding season began,
when the primary enclosure was last cleaned, and when cleaning is expected to resume. Such records must be available
for review by an APHIS inspector.
Correct by 09 April 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.75(b)(1)(iii) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
An acquisition record for a crested pigeon is incomplete. One adult crested pigeon was obtained on 03 February 2026 
from a non-USDA licensed individual and the record did not have the vehicle license number and State, and the driver's 
license number and State of the person. Records containing the required information for animals disposed of, or acquired 
for, use in regulated activities (including those offspring born on premises) are necessary to accurately track animals to 
ensure their humane care and use. The licensee must make and maintain records of the inventory, acquisition and 
disposition of all animals which fully and correctly disclose the required information in this subpart 2.75(b)(1). 
 
Correct from this date forward of 10 March 2026. 
3.155(a) 
   
Feeding 
Multiple slices of white bread with blue-green discoloration suggesting mold activity are on the ground in an enclosure for 
4 domestic chickens. The bread was provided earlier in the day and intended for food. Feeding food with evidence of 
contamination such as mold to birds may cause adverse health effects. The diet for birds must be wholesome, palatable, 
and free of contamination. 
 
Corrected at time of inspection. Correct from this date forward of 10 March 2026. 
3.158(a)(2) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
There are 21 breeding pigeons in an enclosure that has multiple cardboard nests encrusted in droppings. The licensee 
states the cardboard nests are removed when the parents are no longer actively using them and there is no seasonality to 
timing of breeding in this flock. There is no documentation of a modified cleaning schedule or a date when the primary 
enclosure was last cleaned. There is also no documentation of when regular cleaning will resume. 
 
Without a written plan to deviate from normal cleaning, inspectors do not know when the last thorough cleaning was 
performed, what the deviation in cleaning is during breeding, egg-sitting or feeding of chicks, and when normal cleaning

will resume when it no longer may disrupt breeding, egg-sitting, and/or feeding of chicks. Inadequate cleaning can result 
in disease or respiratory irritation in the birds. 
 
Scheduled cleaning may be modified or delayed during breeding, egg-sitting, or feeding of chicks for birds that are easily 
disrupted during such behaviors. Scheduled cleaning must resume when such cleaning no longer disrupts breeding, egg-
sitting, or feeding of chicks. A schedule of cleaning must be documented and must include when breeding season began, 
when the primary enclosure was last cleaned, and when cleaning is expected to resume. Such records must be available 
for review by an APHIS inspector. 
 
Correct by 09 April 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
